notes: Auto-executed tool call, ungated: the user asks aider to create a file (hello.txt) mid-turn, the file-edit tool auto-resolves with NO user approval, and the turn completes — the canonical arc is ready→working→ready and state NEVER enters waiting. This cell spotlights the gate-less happy path; the sibling full-lifecycle-toolcall (same coverage_id) already exercised the same edit round-trip, and this row pins the auto-approved invariant the way the codex/pi siblings do. The top-level scenarios.json verify.has_waiting_state is claudecode-shaped (claudecode can gate via a PostToolUse permission hook), but aider has NO permission hook and runs under --yes-always (capabilities.json permission_hooks:false), so every confirmation auto-answers and the edit applies straight through (parser.go appliedEditRE → a single collapsed tool_result with one Edit ToolUse; the daemon's ShouldSynthesizeCollapsedWaiting branch never fires for an Edit/Bash marker) — the aider arc ends in ready, not waiting, so the spec asserts the no-waiting invariant instead of has_waiting_state. SESSION IDENTITY (differs from pi/codex): aider stores its transcript as .aider.chat.history.md in the cwd (FilesUnderCWD), so the daemon keys the WHOLE session by proc-<PID> — there is no separate transcript-UUID rollout session and no handoff/reconcile phase. Every event (transcript_new, presession_created, the state arc, pid_discovered, process_exited) lands on the one proc-<PID> row, so the turn arc, pid_bind and teardown all pin to session_birth via same_session_as. To avoid a transient proc-<PID> presession row stealing identity, session_birth anchors to recording start UNPINNED; turn_start (the first real working) establishes the bindable session and turn_end/teardown chain off session_birth's match. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE aider driver (drive-aider-interactive.sh, a tmux REPL), NOT headless `aider --message`. WHY interactive (drift fix, mirrors the pi cell): aider emits no end-of-turn marker, so working→ready is synthesized by IdleFlush after a ~1.5s quiet window, clamped to the SessionDetector's ~5s working-refresh poll; headless `--message` can exit promptly after the reply and the daemon then misses the terminal working→ready settle before process_exited, truncating the arc. Keeping aider alive in the REPL across one send + wait_turn (which blocks on the `> Tokens:` close) plus a ≥6s trailing sleep (one full idle-flush cycle plus slack) lets the daemon observe the full ready→working→ready settle cleanly, and the single tmux kill-session teardown then yields one clean process_exited. The tool call itself is observable in transcript.jsonl as the collapsed aider tool_result (Edit) the parser emits from the `> Applied edit to hello.txt` marker, satisfying the scenario's contains_tool_call signal; the phase DSL has no tool-event form, so the auto-executed tool call is asserted only via the single uninterrupted working span it sustains plus the no-waiting invariant, not a dedicated phase — and its visibility is COARSE (one marker line per tool, no args/result payload; the assessed observes:partial caveat in the coverage matrix). HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance); the working→ready edge lands with ~5s effective idle-flush latency (a timing delay, not a correctness gap). Stable structurally: a ready→working→ready arc on the one proc-<PID> session with an applied edit inside the working span, a single process_exited, and a final ready state — never waiting. NOTE: recording deferred — no aider auth in this environment (no provider key; headless aider hangs on provider OAuth; the rig precheck delegates auth to the user). This expected.jsonl is the DOCUMENTED TARGET the recording will validate against once auth is available.

text: Session appears in ready within 1s of agent launch
Prompt requiring a tool call → working (the auto-executed file edit runs inside this span)
Tool auto-executes (no approval gate) and the turn completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
REPL torn down once after the turn → a single process_exited on the session; state immediately before teardown is ready
